turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having some really bad abdominal pain for the past three weeks. It's gotten to the point where I'm getting bloody stools and it's just not stopping, even after I've tried taking Imodium to try and control the diarrhea.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: The crampy lower abdominal pain is pretty constant, but it's always on my left side. The worst part is when I have a bowel movement, because that's when the bloody stools usually occur, and it's just really scary to see blood in my stool.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started about three weeks ago, I'd say. At first, it was just some mild discomfort after meals, but over time it's gotten progressively worse, to the point where I'm up all night with pain and bleeding.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It happens pretty frequently - usually after every bowel movement, which is about 8-10 times a day. It's like my body just can't stop moving, you know?

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: It's an 8 or 9 out of 10, I'd say - the pain is always there, and when I have a bowel movement, it's like someone is stabbing me in the gut. The bleeding doesn't just stop either, it keeps coming even after I've had multiple bowel movements.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: To be honest, yes... I do get a little lightheaded and sweaty when the pain is really bad. It's like my whole body just goes into shock or something. And sometimes, after I've had a bowel movement, I feel like I haven't completely emptied out - it's hard to explain, but it's like there's still stuff in there that needs to come out.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: Actually, yeah... if I eat too much or anything greasy, it tends to make the pain a lot worse. And also, if I'm under a lot of stress, like at work, it seems to trigger more episodes and makes the symptoms even more severe. But it's not just that - sometimes I'll be sitting around doing nothing and suddenly I'll feel this intense cramping and I'm like, "Oh no, here we go again.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: Yeah, I do get a little sweaty when the pain is really bad. And sometimes I feel a bit lightheaded too, but it's not always accompanied by nausea.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: Actually, yeah... my knees and ankles have been hurting a lot lately too. It's just a dull ache, nothing that's really swollen or red, but it's definitely there all the time. I've just assumed it was from working long hours on my feet, but now that you mention it, maybe it's related to what's going on in my gut?

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: No, no swelling at all - just a dull ache. It's actually been bothering me for a while now, but I've just been chalking it up to overwork and not thinking much of it until you mentioned it might be related to my gut issues.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I'm actually taking loperamide, which is Imodium, for the diarrhea. I started taking it about two weeks ago in an attempt to control the symptoms, but it's only given me minimal relief at best. As for other medications, I've been taking ibuprofen a few times a week for my joint pain, but like I said, that seems to make things worse when I'm already experiencing abdominal symptoms.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. I've been feeling pretty healthy otherwise, aside from this whole gut thing going on. My father does have type 2 diabetes, but my mom is fine and I'm not aware of any other conditions that run in my family.

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so... to be honest, I've been so caught up with this gut issue that I haven't really thought about checking my cholesterol or anything else for that matter. My last doctor's visit was over two years ago, and it was just a routine check-up.

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: I don't smoke at all. As for drinking, I do have an occasional beer on weeknights, but I've actually increased my consumption to 4-5 beers per night over the past month because of stress from work and these symptoms. It's not something I'm proud of, but it's just been a way to try and cope with everything that's going on.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient: Honestly, my diet has been pretty terrible lately. With long work hours, I've been relying on takeout and fast food most of the time, which isn't great for digestion, I'm sure. And as for exercise, I try to stay active by walking around during breaks at work, but it's not like I'm going out of my way to get a lot of physical activity outside of that.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease when she was 30, but aside from that, I'm not aware of any other major health issues in my family.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: Actually, my father has type 2 diabetes, and that's about it. My mom is healthy, and like I said, my older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease when she was 30. That's the only serious condition I'm aware of in my family.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: It's been over two years since I last saw a doctor, just a routine check-up and everything seemed fine back then. I've been meaning to go back for follow-ups, but with work and all the stress I've been under lately, it's fallen by the wayside.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. I'm not aware of any medication allergies or sensitivities that I know of.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: Actually, yeah... one more thing. I did travel to Mexico for a work conference about three months ago, but I didn't have any gastrointestinal issues during or after the trip. And I don't recall eating anything unusual that could have triggered this.
